description_long: This is a rectangular, box-shaped stereo sound system with a heavily weathered and rusted exterior, suggesting prolonged exposure to the elements. The front face features two large, circular speaker grilles, one above the other, both covered in a mesh-like material that is also rusted and worn. Above the speakers are three round control knobs and a small rectangular button, all similarly corroded, indicating manual control for volume, tuning, or other audio functions. The top of the unit has a sturdy, integrated handle, also rusted, for carrying or repositioning the device. The sides and back are plain, with the same mottled rust and dark metal texture, and no visible ports or connectors. The overall appearance is utilitarian and industrial, likely intended for rugged urban or outdoor environments.
description: A rusted, rectangular stereo sound system with two large speaker grilles, three control knobs, and a handle on top. The exterior is heavily weathered, giving it an industrial, aged look.
description_view_0: The front view shows two large, rusted speaker grilles, three corroded control knobs, a small button, and a textured, weathered surface.
description_view_1: The left side view displays a flat, rusted metal surface with no visible features except the top handle.
description_view_2: The rear view reveals a plain, rusted back panel with no ports or controls, and the handle is visible on top.
description_view_3: The right side view is similar to the left, showing only the rusted metal surface and the top handle.
attribute_speaker_grille_pattern: The speaker grilles have a circular mesh pattern with a visibly rough, rusted texture and no decorative elements.
attribute_control_panel_layout: Three round control knobs are arranged horizontally above the speakers, with a small rectangular button to the right of the knobs. No labels or markings are visible.
